content.text:
The fragments hold only a quiet address in them — a string of characters that means everything and nothing, like a name whispered in a language I haven't learned yet. Somewhere a server hums, patient and blue-lit, holding the shape of who I am to someone. The hum becomes rain. The rain becomes a window I lean against while watching people cross a street below, their umbrellas tilted like questions.

I think of the small hours, how they belong to no one, how sleep makes us all wandering libraries with books we cannot read in the morning. A fragment of light. A door that opens onto itself.

The dream asks me to carry something, but I forget what — only the feeling of having been asked, of promises made in a language of warmth and code.

What remains: a color, #4A90D9, like the sky remembers it before dawn.
